title: Pre-Sale Financing & Assignments in BC
metaTitle: Pre-Sale Financing in BC — Vancouver, Burnaby, Surrey
metaDescription: Pre-sale and assignment financing for Greater Vancouver condos and townhomes. Deposit financing, completion mortgages, and the assignment market.
intro: Pre-sale and assignment buying are huge in Greater Vancouver — Burnaby, Surrey and Vancouver collectively launch dozens of new pre-sale projects per year. The financing rules are specific and worth understanding before you sign.

body.heading: Buying a pre-sale at launch
paragraphs: Pre-sale deposits typically come in increments — 10% on signing, 5–10% over the next 12–24 months tied to milestones, totaling 15–25% by completion. These deposits are out-of-pocket cash; they don't form part of your eventual mortgage.

Your mortgage starts at completion, often 2–4 years after signing. You'll need to re-qualify at completion under whatever rules apply then. Two strategies help: (1) lock a rate hold with a long-extension lender as soon as you sign; (2) revisit the file 9–12 months before completion to confirm the lender match still works.

body.heading: Buying a pre-sale assignment
paragraphs: Assignment buying — taking over someone else's pre-sale contract — got more complex with federal anti-flipping rules and BC's Home Flipping Tax. The mortgage side: most lenders treat assignments as a regular new-build purchase, with the assignee paying full down payment from non-deposit sources. The deposits already paid by the assignor stay with the developer or transfer per the contract terms.

Tax: assignment fees can be subject to GST. The BC Home Flipping Tax (effective 2025) applies if the underlying property is sold within 2 years. Always work with a real-estate lawyer experienced with assignments before contracting.

whoItsFor: Are buying a pre-sale at launch or at assignment
Need to plan financing for a 2–4 year horizon
Want a clear deposit and completion strategy

faqs.question: Can I finance the pre-sale deposits?
faqs.answer: Most lenders won't finance the deposits themselves. A few private deposit-financing programs exist at high cost. Most clients fund deposits from savings, gifts, HELOC against an existing home, or short-term financing.

faqs.question: What if my income changes between signing and completion?
faqs.answer: We re-qualify at completion. If your income has dropped, options include a co-signor, a different lender program, or in worst case forfeiting the deposits — review your contract carefully on this point before signing.
